title: Annual leave — cashing out and directing

meta.description: Rules for cashing out annual leave, directing employees to take leave, and accrual.

headings.text: When can annual leave be cashed out?
Directing an employee to take annual leave
Direction to take leave during shutdown

content:
Annual leave accrual:
Full-time and part-time employees accrue 4 weeks of paid annual leave for each year of service (pro-rata for part-time employees based on ordinary hours). Shiftworkers may accrue 5 weeks under some awards. Annual leave accrues progressively throughout the year and accumulates year-to-year if unused.

Casual employees are NOT entitled to paid annual leave.

Cashing out annual leave:
An award or enterprise agreement must allow cashing out. Not all awards permit it. Where permitted, the following conditions must be met:
- The employee must retain at least 4 weeks of annual leave after cashing out
- A separate written agreement must be made each time annual leave is cashed out (a blanket agreement is not sufficient)
- The employee must be paid the same amount they would have received if they had taken the leave

An employer cannot pressure or coerce an employee to cash out their leave.

Directing an employee to take annual leave:
An employer can direct an employee to take annual leave in certain circumstances, depending on what the award or enterprise agreement says. Common situations:

1. Excess annual leave direction:
- If an employee has accumulated excess annual leave (often 8 weeks or more), some awards allow the employer to direct them to take leave
- The direction must give reasonable notice (often 8 weeks' written notice)
- Leave directed must be at least 1 week long
- Generally cannot result in the employee having less than 6 weeks of accrued leave remaining

2. Direction during a shutdown:
- An award or enterprise agreement may allow an employer to direct employees to take annual leave during a shutdown (e.g. over Christmas/New Year)
- The direction must be reasonable and provide reasonable notice
- If an employee doesn't have sufficient leave balance, the employer and employee can agree to the employee taking leave in advance or unpaid leave

Leave loading:
Some awards and enterprise agreements provide for annual leave loading — an extra 17.5% payment on top of ordinary pay while on annual leave. Check the applicable award to confirm if leave loading applies.
tables: 4 weeks per year (full-time); pro-rata for part-time

Same rate as if leave was taken
